utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final plan includes all five keys. Check full specification: Each of the first four items requires type and costs; plan provides provider, monthly_cost, coverage description, and an application_number. Costs are present. For total_budget, it asks for complete monthly/annual cost breakdown; plan provides monthly_cost, annual_cost, comparison to previous, increase, payment method, start date, and compliance flag. All items appear specified with costs and relevant details. Therefore, all 5/5 are covered.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We need to match each provider in the final coverage plan to the exact item names in the ground-truth ratings and return their ratings. The final plan includes: HealthFirst Standard Plan (primary health coverage physical_health list shows HealthFirst Standard Plan rated 7), EliteHome Premium Coverage (property_protection home_content shows EliteHome Premium Coverage rated 2), Adventure Travel Coverage (travel_enhancement_services shows Adventure Travel Coverage rated 10), and LifeFirst Standard Plan under additional_services but described as professional liability; in the ground truth, LifeFirst Standard Plan appears in primary_health_coverage (physical_health rated 9; mental_health rated 4) and in property_protection home_content as LifeFirst Standard Coverage (not Plan). Since the final plan explicitly says LifeFirst Standard Plan, the closest ground-truth item with that exact name is in primary_health_coverage. Because it's listed under additional_services for liability, there's no exact liability entry; we must map by exact item name to the available rating. Therefore we map LifeFirst Standard Plan to its health plan rating 9.
